as_of.title: Navigating Uncertainty: Inflation, Labor Markets, and the Stance of Monetary Policy

inflation.label: oil + tariffs
inflation.note: She frames elevated inflation as driven by classic supply shocks from tariffs and the Middle East energy/commodity spike, with long-run expectations still anchored.
inflation.quote: Most recently, tariffs and the spike in energy and commodity prices associated with the conflict in the Middle East are pushing inflation higher.

labor.label: stable / balanced
labor.note: Hard data show a labor market stable and consistent with full employment near 4.3 percent, despite softer survey sentiment.
labor.quote: the hard data suggest a labor market that is stable and consistent with full employment.

stance.label: somewhat/modestly restrictive
stance.note: She views the current stance as appropriate and well-positioned, with that restrictiveness helping keep supply-shock inflation in check.
stance.quote: Policy is mildly restrictive and that restrictiveness is helping to keep inflation pressures in check while the labor market remains stable.
